mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding the long-term trend rate signals robust aggregate demand, which directly boosts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in high-GDP-growth periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating federal funds rate hikes compress equity valuation multiples by raising the discount rate applied to future cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the rate acceleration signal, or forward returns show no statistically significant difference from non-triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous payroll growth and declining unemployment confirm a strengthening labor market, which supports consumer spending and corporate revenue growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when tested against a random baseline over a 3-year rolling window, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression yields a p-value greater than 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side efficiency gains, which lower input costs and improve profit margins, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative when INDPRO rises above its 12-month moving average, or the signal's predictive power vanishes during periods of supply chain disruption.

mechanism_narrative: Increasing housing starts reflect rising consumer confidence and credit availability, which stimulate related economic activity and boost equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Housing starts decline while SPX 20d returns remain positive, or the correlation between HOUST changes and forward returns drops below 0.05 over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained drop in the unemployment rate below 5.0 percent signals a tight labor market that fuels wage growth and consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are negative or flat when UNRATE crosses below 5.0%, or the signal generates more false positives than true positives in a 10-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation outpacing the federal funds rate creates a negative real interest rate environment that erodes corporate profit margins and increases borrowing costs, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive despite widening inflation-rate gaps, or the interaction effect between CPI and FEDFUNDS shows no statistical significance in a rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: Growing residential mortgage debt outstanding indicates expanding household leverage and credit access, which fuels consumption and supports corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: RSAFS growth decouples from equity returns during periods of financial stress, or the signal's information coefficient falls below 0.02 over any 12-month window.
